title: Actinocephalus trichopeplus
taxonomy: Actinocephalus trichopeplus is classified within the family Eriocaulaceae. This family is a part of the order Poales. The order Poales is part of the class Liliopsida, also known as monocots. The class Liliopsida is a subgroup of the larger division Magnoliophyta, which includes flowering plants. At the highest taxonomic level, Actinocephalus trichopeplus is a member of the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Actinocephalus trichopeplus is a species of flowering plant in the family Eriocaulaceae, commonly known as a type of hair grass or pipit grass. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Brazil, to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Actinocephalus trichopeplus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Actinocephalus trichopeplus is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
